AD_MODULE.NAME: Recurring Transactions
AD_MODULE.DESCRIPTION: Define recurring or repeating transactions, such as recurring G/L journal entries, recurring invoices, recurring financial account transactions, etc.
AD_MODULE.HELP:
This module allows to automate the creation of recurring or repeating transactions.
For example a monthly payment into a professional insurance requires the same transaction to be entered, without variation, month after month. This module automates the creation of these transactions.
AD_MODULE.LICENSE: For a copy of the license, please visit http://www.openbravo.com/license
AD_MODULE.AUTHOR: Openbravo S.L.U.
AD_MODULE.UPDATEINFO: == 0.4.0 == Release candidate version with limited functionality. Suitable for production usage. This version supports recurring transactions for G/L Journal only. Refactored code to handle transaction consistency across commits and eliminate errors in the process. Fixed issues: 005: For a rule with more than one template, if one fails all are skipped 006: Rules fail when complete is checked == 0.3.1 == Fixed issue 004: When multiple templates are in use and the first one is skipped, all are skipped == 0.3.0 == Release candidate version with limited functionality. Suitable for production usage. This version supports recurring transactions for G/L Journal only. Recurring transactions can now be completed automatically. == 0.2.3 == Fixed issues: 2. When a completed template is cloned and the rule does not complete, the recurring clone should not be completed 3. Total dr and total cr are doubled for cloned GL Journal header == 0.2.2 == Fixed issues: 1 Transactions are not getting cloned == 0.2.0: RC1 == Release candidate version with limited functionality. Suitable for production usage. This version supports recurring transactions for G/L Journal only. Recurring transactions do not complete. Daily, Monthly, Quarterly and Yearly recurrence is supported. == 0.1.1: Development release ==
AD_MODULE.REFERENCEDATAINFO: Recurring template types: system level definition of the logic to clone recurring transactions
